HomePortfolioSpeedZone
Turnkey Project Case Study

SpeedZone Family Park

Complete 800m outdoor karting attraction — from site survey to grand opening in under 6 months.

850m
Track Length
40
EV Karts
5mo
Timeline
$3.2M
Project Value
Project Overview

From Empty Land to Opening Day

SpeedZone Family Park approached VortKart® with a greenfield site and a vision for a world-class karting attraction. Starting with nothing but a flat plot of land, our team delivered a complete operational facility — track, karts, barriers, timing, signage, and staff training — in under 6 months.

The 800-meter circuit was designed to accommodate 30 EV-Pro® 48V electric karts simultaneously, with a layout that maximized both track time per session and the spectator experience for non-riders. The result was an attraction that exceeded revenue projections in its first year of operation.

"VortKart® designed our entire 800m track from scratch, supplied 30 electric karts, and had us operational in under 6 months. The ROI exceeded our projections in year one. We couldn't have asked for a better partner."

JM
James Mitchell
Owner, SpeedZone Family Park, USA

Project Facts

ClientSpeedZone Family Park
LocationCalifornia, USA
Project TypeTurnkey Attraction
Kart ModelEV-Pro® 48V
Fleet Size30 Units
Track Length800 Metres
Track TypeOutdoor Asphalt
Delivery Time< 6 Months
CompletedMarch 2024
Start Similar Project
Scope of Work

What We Delivered

Track Design & Construction

850m FIA-spec circuit with 12 corners, elevation changes, drainage, and asphalt paving to motorsport-grade tolerances.

Fleet Manufacturing & Delivery

40 custom-liveried EV-Pro® 48 electric karts manufactured, tested, and delivered with full spare parts package.

Timing & Scoring System

Professional AMB timing system with digital leaderboard, driver performance printouts, and cloud-based session management.

Facility Infrastructure

Pit lane, grid area, spectator grandstand (200 seats), driver briefing room, and maintenance workshop construction.

Staff Training & Certification

2-week on-site training for 12 staff covering operations, safety, kart maintenance, and emergency procedures.

Post-Launch Support

12-month on-call technical support, quarterly maintenance visits, and remote fleet monitoring via VortKart Connect™.

Fleet Deployment

Karts & Equipment

ItemQuantityNotes
EV-Pro® 48 Electric Kart40 unitsPrimary rental fleet
JuniorSprint® EV8 unitsJunior track (Phase 2)
AMB Timing Transponders48 unitsKart-mounted
Fast Charge Stations6 units45-min DC fast charge
Spare Parts Package1 set2-year maintenance stock
Project Timeline

From Concept to Completion

Month 1

Site Survey & Design

Geotechnical survey, track layout design, permits, and civil engineering plans finalized.

Month 2

Groundbreaking & Earthworks

Site clearing, grading, drainage installation, and track bed preparation completed.

Month 3

Track Paving & Infrastructure

Asphalt paving, barrier installation, pit lane construction, and facility build-out.

Month 4

Fleet Delivery & System Install

40 EV-Pro karts delivered, timing system installed, charging stations commissioned.

Month 5

Training, Testing & Launch

Staff training, safety certification, soft-opening test events, and public grand opening.
